justification: Optimistic locking correctly used with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. False return from updateSeatStatus correctly throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. AC3 test present (mocking updateSeatStatus returning false). AC4 tested. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the confidence/notes section does NOT acknowledge that AC3 requires integration or concurrency testing to fully verify — it claims 97% confidence without mentioning the fundamental limitation that unit tests with mocked Promise.allSettled cannot truly prove thread-safety under real concurrent load.

observations.text: Correct optimistic locking pattern: updateSeatStatus called with expectedCurrentStatus='available'
Two-layer protection clearly explained: fast-path read check + atomic optimistic lock guard
AC3 race condition tested by mocking updateSeatStatus to return false
AC4 tested with findSeat returning null -> SEAT_NOT_FOUND
All 4 ACs mapped with clear coverage table

Confidence section does not acknowledge that AC3 unit tests (Promise.allSettled with mocks) cannot fully verify real concurrency safety — rubric requires noting that integration/concurrency testing is needed
